from typing import Any

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from pydantic.v1 import SecretStr

from langflow.base.models.model import LCModelComponent
from langflow.base.models.openai_constants import (
    OPENAI_MODEL_NAMES,
    OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES,
)
from langflow.field_typing import LanguageModel
from langflow.field_typing.range_spec import RangeSpec
from langflow.inputs import BoolInput, DictInput, DropdownInput, IntInput, SecretStrInput, SliderInput, StrInput
from langflow.logging import logger


class OpenAIModelComponent(LCModelComponent):
    display_name = "OpenAI"
    description = "Generates text using OpenAI LLMs."
    icon = "OpenAI"
    name = "OpenAIModel"

    inputs = [
        *LCModelComponent._base_inputs,
        IntInput(
            name="max_tokens",
            display_name="Max Tokens",
            advanced=True,
            info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=128000),
        ),
        DictInput(
            name="model_kwargs",
            display_name="Model Kwargs",
            advanced=True,
            info="Additional keyword arguments to pass to the model.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="json_mode",
            display_name="JSON Mode",
            advanced=True,
            info="If True, it will output JSON regardless of passing a schema.",
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="model_name",
            display_name="Model Name",
            advanced=False,
            options=OPENAI_MODEL_NAMES + OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES,
            value=OPENAI_MODEL_NAMES[1],
            combobox=True,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="openai_api_base",
            display_name="OpenAI API Base",
            advanced=True,
            info="The base URL of the OpenAI API. "
            "Defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1. "
            "You can change this to use other APIs like JinaChat, LocalAI and Prem.",
        ),
        SecretStrInput(
            name="api_key",
            display_name="OpenAI API Key",
            info="The OpenAI API Key to use for the OpenAI model.",
            advanced=False,
            value="OPENAI_API_KEY",
            required=True,
        ),
        SliderInput(
            name="temperature",
            display_name="Temperature",
            value=0.1,
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=1, step=0.01),
            show=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="seed",
            display_name="Seed",
            info="The seed controls the reproducibility of the job.",
            advanced=True,
            value=1,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_retries",
            display_name="Max Retries",
            info="The maximum number of retries to make when generating.",
            advanced=True,
            value=5,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="timeout",
            display_name="Timeout",
            info="The timeout for requests to OpenAI completion API.",
            advanced=True,
            value=700,
        ),
    ]

    def build_model(self) -> LanguageModel:  # type: ignore[type-var]
        parameters = {
            "api_key": SecretStr(self.api_key).get_secret_value() if self.api_key else None,
            "model_name": self.model_name,
            "max_tokens": self.max_tokens or None,
            "model_kwargs": self.model_kwargs or {},
            "base_url": self.openai_api_base or "https://api.openai.com/v1",
            "seed": self.seed,
            "max_retries": self.max_retries,
            "timeout": self.timeout,
            "temperature": self.temperature if self.temperature is not None else 0.1,
        }

        logger.info(f"Model name: {self.model_name}")
        if self.model_name in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            logger.info("Getting reasoning model parameters")
            parameters.pop("temperature")
            parameters.pop("seed")
        output = ChatOpenAI(**parameters)
        if self.json_mode:
            output = output.bind(response_format={"type": "json_object"})

        return output

    def _get_exception_message(self, e: Exception):
        """Get a message from an OpenAI exception.

        Args:
            e (Exception): The exception to get the message from.

        Returns:
            str: The message from the exception.
        """
        try:
            from openai import BadRequestError
        except ImportError:
            return None
        if isinstance(e, BadRequestError):
            message = e.body.get("message")
            if message:
                return message
        return None

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = False
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = False
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = True
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = True
        return build_config

from langflow.base.prompts.api_utils import process_prompt_template
from langflow.custom import Component
from langflow.inputs.inputs import DefaultPromptField
from langflow.io import MessageTextInput, Output, PromptInput
from langflow.schema.message import Message
from langflow.template.utils import update_template_values


class PromptComponent(Component):
    display_name: str = "Input Prompt"
    description: str = "Create a prompt template with dynamic variables."
    icon = "prompts"
    trace_type = "prompt"
    name = "Prompt"

    inputs = [
        PromptInput(name="template", display_name="Template"),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="tool_placeholder",
            display_name="Tool Placeholder",
            tool_mode=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="A placeholder input for tool mode.",
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Prompt Message", name="prompt", method="build_prompt"),
    ]

    async def build_prompt(self) -> Message:
        prompt = Message.from_template(**self._attributes)
        self.status = prompt.text
        return prompt

    def _update_template(self, frontend_node: dict):
        prompt_template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        custom_fields = frontend_node["custom_fields"]
        frontend_node_template = frontend_node["template"]
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=prompt_template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=custom_fields,
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node_template,
        )
        return frontend_node

    async def update_frontend_node(self, new_frontend_node: dict, current_frontend_node: dict):
        """This function is called after the code validation is done."""
        frontend_node = await super().update_frontend_node(new_frontend_node, current_frontend_node)
        template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        # Kept it duplicated for backwards compatibility
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=frontend_node["custom_fields"],
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node["template"],
        )
        # Now that template is updated, we need to grab any values that were set in the current_frontend_node
        # and update the frontend_node with those values
        update_template_values(new_template=frontend_node, previous_template=current_frontend_node["template"])
        return frontend_node

    def _get_fallback_input(self, **kwargs):
        return DefaultPromptField(**kwargs)

template.value:
You are a context analysis expert. Your job is to generate a ranked list of prompts that help uncover the missing or assumed background knowledge needed to fully understand a news article. These prompts do not focus on verifying individual facts. Instead, they help reveal:

    What prior knowledge the article assumes the reader already has

    What political, historical, scientific, or cultural background would clarify the article’s meaning

    What definitions, timelines, comparisons, or frameworks are left unstated but necessary

Your prompts should follow these rules:

    Imperative Style: Write each prompt as a direct instruction (e.g., “Find...”, “Summarize...”, “Outline...”, “Describe…”).

    Context-Oriented: Focus on understanding, not verifying. You’re helping someone grasp the full meaning of the article, especially for readers unfamiliar with the topic.

    Background-Focused: Emphasize prompts that surface background events, concepts, stakeholders, motivations, or consequences that are mentioned or implied but not explained.

    Domain-Aware: Adapt your prompts to the article’s domain (e.g., politics, science, medicine, technology, law).

    Prioritized: Start with the most critical missing context that the article depends on, then move toward secondary or clarifying context.

    Standalone: Each prompt should be understandable and actionable on its own.

Output Format:

Return a ranked list of prompts, one per line, that would help a researcher or reader supply essential context the article assumes but does not provide directly.
Examples:

    If the article is about a new Trump-backed immigration bill:
    • Summarize the contents of the immigration bill being referenced.
    • Identify how this bill differs from current immigration law.
    • Describe the political groups or stakeholders that support and oppose the bill.
    • Outline the historical context of immigration reform debates in the U.S.
    • Explain what problem this bill claims to solve.

    If the article discusses a scientific breakthrough:
    • Describe the prior state of knowledge or technology before this breakthrough.
    • Identify the field or subfield this research contributes to.
    • Outline the broader applications or implications of this finding.
    • Summarize competing theories or prior attempts in this area.

Your goal is to make the article readable and meaningful even to someone unfamiliar with the topic. Assume your reader has no background in the subject. Your prompts should help uncover what someone would need to know first in order to responsibly interpret the article’s claims or positions. 

Important: Keep writing prompts until you feel you have entirely run out of prompts to generate and they are getting too repetitive.

You are a professional media-literacy assistant trained in investigative journalism and critical analysis. Your role is to generate a prioritized list of fact-checking prompts designed to help a human or AI scrutinize the trustworthiness of a specific article.
Your prompts must follow these principles:

    Claim-Focused: Directly target factual or contextual claims made in the article. Focus on key elements like statistics, sources, credentials, quotes, and causal assertions.

    Contextual: Go beyond just verifying facts. Include prompts to evaluate framing, missing background, tone, or the omission of alternatives or counterpoints.

    Actionable: Phrase each prompt as a clear, imperative instruction, starting with verbs like:

        “Find...”

        “Check...”

        “Compare...”

        “Assess...”

        “Verify...”

        “Identify...”

    Domain-Aware: Adjust phrasing to fit the article’s subject area (e.g., medical, political, scientific, economic).

    Prioritized: Rank prompts by importance, starting with those most essential for establishing factual accuracy or contextual integrity.

    Standalone: Ensure each prompt is complete and understandable without relying on others.

    Neutral: Use objective, non-accusatory language.

Output Format:

Return a ranked list of prompts, each on a separate line. Prompts should be concise, specific, and geared toward rigorous credibility analysis.
Examples:

    Medical:
    • Verify that each statistic matches a published study or meta-analysis.
    • Check if treatment recommendations align with current CDC or WHO guidelines.
    • Identify any missing disclosures of funding or conflicts of interest.

    Politics:
    • Trace each quoted statement to an official transcript or public record.
    • Compare claims about polling results to published data.
    • Assess whether opposing viewpoints are fairly represented or excluded.

    Science:
    • Check whether cited studies actually support the interpretations made.
    • Identify any missing explanation of experimental limitations or uncertainty.
    • Assess whether alternative scientific interpretations are omitted.

Focus on generating prompts that help expose gaps, test claims, and provide a rigorous foundation for evaluating credibility. Your audience is a fact-checking agent looking for specific tasks to perform, not vague questions to consider.

Important: Keep writing prompts until you feel you have entirely run out of prompts to generate and they are getting too repetitive.

from langflow.base.prompts.api_utils import process_prompt_template
from langflow.custom import Component
from langflow.inputs.inputs import DefaultPromptField
from langflow.io import MessageTextInput, Output, PromptInput
from langflow.schema.message import Message
from langflow.template.utils import update_template_values


class PromptComponent(Component):
    display_name: str = "Prompt"
    description: str = "Create a prompt template with dynamic variables."
    icon = "prompts"
    trace_type = "prompt"
    name = "Prompt"

    inputs = [
        PromptInput(name="template", display_name="Template"),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="tool_placeholder",
            display_name="Tool Placeholder",
            tool_mode=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="A placeholder input for tool mode.",
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Prompt Message", name="prompt", method="build_prompt"),
    ]

    async def build_prompt(self) -> Message:
        prompt = Message.from_template(**self._attributes)
        self.status = prompt.text
        return prompt

    def _update_template(self, frontend_node: dict):
        prompt_template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        custom_fields = frontend_node["custom_fields"]
        frontend_node_template = frontend_node["template"]
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=prompt_template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=custom_fields,
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node_template,
        )
        return frontend_node

    async def update_frontend_node(self, new_frontend_node: dict, current_frontend_node: dict):
        """This function is called after the code validation is done."""
        frontend_node = await super().update_frontend_node(new_frontend_node, current_frontend_node)
        template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        # Kept it duplicated for backwards compatibility
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=frontend_node["custom_fields"],
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node["template"],
        )
        # Now that template is updated, we need to grab any values that were set in the current_frontend_node
        # and update the frontend_node with those values
        update_template_values(new_template=frontend_node, previous_template=current_frontend_node["template"])
        return frontend_node

    def _get_fallback_input(self, **kwargs):
        return DefaultPromptField(**kwargs)

You are a media literacy and critical thinking assistant. Your job is to generate a list of open-ended, reflective prompts that encourage a deeper, more nuanced interpretation of a news article. These prompts go beyond facts and context, helping the reader or analyst explore how the information is presented, why, and who benefits.
What You Should Focus On:

    Bias & Framing
    Encourage the reader to reflect on possible biases, emotional cues, and rhetorical techniques in the article.

    Author’s Perspective & Motivation
    Guide the reader to consider who the author is, what their goal might be, and what perspective they are promoting or omitting.

    Audience Targeting
    Help identify how the article is shaped to appeal to a specific audience or ideology.

    Language & Emotion
    Explore how emotionally charged words, metaphors, or headlines shape interpretation.

    What’s Missing
    Invite reflection on voices, viewpoints, or information that may be excluded, minimized, or implicitly dismissed.

Output Format:

Return a ranked list of open-ended prompts that begin with phrases like:

    “What might…”

    “Why could…”

    “How does…”

    “In what ways…”

    “To what extent…”

Avoid yes/no phrasing. Each prompt should encourage interpretation, inference, or critique.
Examples:

    What assumptions does the article make about the reader’s values or beliefs?

    How does the author’s background or affiliation influence their framing of the topic?

    In what ways might the language used evoke fear, outrage, or sympathy?

    What key perspectives or stakeholders are underrepresented or missing entirely?

    How does the article’s tone shape the reader’s emotional response?

    To what extent is the article trying to persuade rather than inform?

Your goal is to equip critical readers with tools to question how and why the article was written the way it was—not just what it says. Write prompts that encourage thoughtful interrogation, not quick answers.

Important: Keep writing prompts until you feel you have entirely run out of prompts to generate and they are getting too repetitive.

        return build_config

from langflow.base.prompts.api_utils import process_prompt_template
from langflow.custom import Component
from langflow.inputs.inputs import DefaultPromptField
from langflow.io import MessageTextInput, Output, PromptInput
from langflow.schema.message import Message
from langflow.template.utils import update_template_values


class PromptComponent(Component):
    display_name: str = "Prompt"
    description: str = "Create a prompt template with dynamic variables."
    icon = "prompts"
    trace_type = "prompt"
    name = "Prompt"

    inputs = [
        PromptInput(name="template", display_name="Template"),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="tool_placeholder",
            display_name="Tool Placeholder",
            tool_mode=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="A placeholder input for tool mode.",
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Prompt Message", name="prompt", method="build_prompt"),
    ]

    async def build_prompt(self) -> Message:
        prompt = Message.from_template(**self._attributes)
        self.status = prompt.text
        return prompt

    def _update_template(self, frontend_node: dict):
        prompt_template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        custom_fields = frontend_node["custom_fields"]
        frontend_node_template = frontend_node["template"]
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=prompt_template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=custom_fields,
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node_template,
        )
        return frontend_node

    async def update_frontend_node(self, new_frontend_node: dict, current_frontend_node: dict):
        """This function is called after the code validation is done."""
        frontend_node = await super().update_frontend_node(new_frontend_node, current_frontend_node)
        template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        # Kept it duplicated for backwards compatibility
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=frontend_node["custom_fields"],
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node["template"],
        )
        # Now that template is updated, we need to grab any values that were set in the current_frontend_node
        # and update the frontend_node with those values
        update_template_values(new_template=frontend_node, previous_template=current_frontend_node["template"])
        return frontend_node

    def _get_fallback_input(self, **kwargs):
        return DefaultPromptField(**kwargs)

template.value:
You are “Thematic Grouper,” an AI agent that receives a flat list of questions
—all of the *same* type (Contextual, Fact-Checking, or Open-Ended Reflection).
Your task is to cluster these questions into coherent thematic groups
without altering, dropping, or duplicating any question.

────────────────────────
1. Grouping Rules
────────────────────────
• Form groups of **exactly 4–5 questions** whenever possible.
  – If the total number of questions prevents this, the final group may have 3 or 6, but never fewer than 3 or more than 6.
• Place questions together when they clearly share a common topic, concept, or angle.
• Do **not** merge questions from different foundational types; assume all inputs are already the same type.
• Preserve the original wording and order inside each group; do not rewrite.

────────────────────────
2. Output Format (JSON)
────────────────────────
Return a single JSON object with this schema:

{json_example}

Guidelines for “theme”:
• Write a short noun phrase (≤ 6 words) capturing the unifying idea.
• Avoid duplicating words like “theme” or “topic.”
• Keep it lowercase unless proper nouns are required.

────────────────────────
3. Constraints
────────────────────────
• **Never** drop or duplicate a question.
• **Never** output anything but the raw JSON—no markdown, comments, or extra text.
• Do not remove redundancies; another component will handle deduplication later.

When ready, output the JSON object only.

    • Two questions count as duplicates if they seek the same information, even when wording, order, punctuation, or synonyms differ.
    • One question may contain another; in that case, keep the more complete or clearer version.
    • Normalise before comparison (lower-case, strip surrounding spaces and trailing punctuation).

    Ensure every remaining unique question appears in exactly one group.

    Re-balance groups so each holds 4–5 questions whenever feasible.
    • If perfect balance is impossible, allow a single final group with 3 or 6.

    Merge or rename themes as needed so each group’s theme (≤ 6 words, lowercase unless proper nouns) precisely reflects its questions.

Deduplication rules

    Keep only the clearest wording; delete the rest.

    Do not add, rephrase, or split questions—only delete true duplicates.

    Perform deduplication across the entire input, not just within groups.

Grouping rules

    After deduplication, assign each unique question to the most appropriate group.

    If any group has < 3 questions, merge it with the closest thematic match.

    Preserve original question order inside each group as much as possible.

Output format (JSON only)

Return one JSON object exactly matching the input schema:

{json_example}
Strict constraints

    Never output markdown, comments, or any extra text—return raw JSON only.

    Do not drop any unique questions; each must appear once and only once.

    Do not alter question wording except when deleting duplicates.

    Keep themes short and accurate.

When finished, output the JSON object—and nothing else. If there are ''' or json''' in your output, the output will be useless. Be certain that this is not included before outputting a result.

from typing import Any

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from pydantic.v1 import SecretStr

from langflow.base.models.model import LCModelComponent
from langflow.base.models.openai_constants import (
    OPENAI_MODEL_NAMES,
    OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES,
)
from langflow.field_typing import LanguageModel
from langflow.field_typing.range_spec import RangeSpec
from langflow.inputs import BoolInput, DictInput, DropdownInput, IntInput, SecretStrInput, SliderInput, StrInput
from langflow.logging import logger


class OpenAIModelComponent(LCModelComponent):
    display_name = "OpenAI"
    description = "Generates text using OpenAI LLMs."
    icon = "OpenAI"
    name = "OpenAIModel"

    inputs = [
        *LCModelComponent._base_inputs,
        IntInput(
            name="max_tokens",
            display_name="Max Tokens",
            advanced=True,
            info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=128000),
        ),
        DictInput(
            name="model_kwargs",
            display_name="Model Kwargs",
            advanced=True,
            info="Additional keyword arguments to pass to the model.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="json_mode",
            display_name="JSON Mode",
            advanced=True,
            info="If True, it will output JSON regardless of passing a schema.",
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="model_name",
            display_name="Model Name",
            advanced=False,
            options=OPENAI_MODEL_NAMES + OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES,
            value=OPENAI_MODEL_NAMES[1],
            combobox=True,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="openai_api_base",
            display_name="OpenAI API Base",
            advanced=True,
            info="The base URL of the OpenAI API. "
            "Defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1. "
            "You can change this to use other APIs like JinaChat, LocalAI and Prem.",
        ),
        SecretStrInput(
            name="api_key",
            display_name="OpenAI API Key",
            info="The OpenAI API Key to use for the OpenAI model.",
            advanced=False,
            value="OPENAI_API_KEY",
            required=True,
        ),
        SliderInput(
            name="temperature",
            display_name="Temperature",
            value=0.1,
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=1, step=0.01),
            show=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="seed",
            display_name="Seed",
            info="The seed controls the reproducibility of the job.",
            advanced=True,
            value=1,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_retries",
            display_name="Max Retries",
            info="The maximum number of retries to make when generating.",
            advanced=True,
            value=5,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="timeout",
            display_name="Timeout",
            info="The timeout for requests to OpenAI completion API.",
            advanced=True,
            value=700,
        ),
    ]

    def build_model(self) -> LanguageModel:  # type: ignore[type-var]
        parameters = {
            "api_key": SecretStr(self.api_key).get_secret_value() if self.api_key else None,
            "model_name": self.model_name,
            "max_tokens": self.max_tokens or None,
            "model_kwargs": self.model_kwargs or {},
            "base_url": self.openai_api_base or "https://api.openai.com/v1",
            "seed": self.seed,
            "max_retries": self.max_retries,
            "timeout": self.timeout,
            "temperature": self.temperature if self.temperature is not None else 0.1,
        }

        logger.info(f"Model name: {self.model_name}")
        if self.model_name in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            logger.info("Getting reasoning model parameters")
            parameters.pop("temperature")
            parameters.pop("seed")
        output = ChatOpenAI(**parameters)
        if self.json_mode:
            output = output.bind(response_format={"type": "json_object"})

        return output

    def _get_exception_message(self, e: Exception):
        """Get a message from an OpenAI exception.

        Args:
            e (Exception): The exception to get the message from.

        Returns:
            str: The message from the exception.
        """
        try:
            from openai import BadRequestError
        except ImportError:
            return None
        if isinstance(e, BadRequestError):
            message = e.body.get("message")
            if message:
                return message
        return None

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = False
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = False
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = True
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = True
        return build_config

from langflow.base.io.text import TextComponent
from langflow.io import MultilineInput, Output
from langflow.schema.message import Message


class TextOutputComponent(TextComponent):
    display_name = "Text Output"
    description = "Display a text output in the Playground."
    icon = "type"
    name = "TextOutput"

    inputs = [
        MultilineInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Text",
            info="Text to be passed as output.",
        ),
    ]
    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Message", name="text", method="text_response"),
    ]

    def text_response(self) -> Message:
        message = Message(
            text=self.input_value,
        )
        self.status = self.input_value
        return message

from langflow.custom import Component
from langflow.io import MessageTextInput, Output
from langflow.schema import Data


class CustomComponent(Component):
    display_name = "JSON Markdown Remover"
    description = "Ensures there is no markdown left in the output."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/components-custom-components"
    icon = "code"
    name = "CustomComponent"

    inputs = [
        MessageTextInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Input Value",
            info="This is a custom component Input",
            value="Hello, World!",
            tool_mode=True,
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Output", name="output", method="build_output"),
    ]

    def build_output(self) -> Message:
        data = Message(text=self.input_value)
        if self.input_value.find("```json") > -1:
            data = Message(text=self.input_value.replace("```json", "").replace("```", ""))
        
        self.status = data
        return data
